item.title: Puig, Estee Lauder founding families to meet for combination talks, source says
item.description: The founding ‌families of the Spanish firm Puig , which owns such brands as Rabanne and Nina Ricci, and U.S. cosmetics giant Estee Lauder will meet this week in New York to negotiate the ​terms of a potential business combination, a person with knowledge of the ​talks said on Tuesday.

item.title: Novo Nordisk launches higher-dose Wegovy in the United States
item.description: Novo Nordisk said on Tuesday that it has made a higher-dose ​version of its blockbuster weight-loss drug ‌Wegovy available across the United States.

item.title: US stock futures inch lower as investors assess Mideast developments
item.description: U.S. stock index futures ticked lower on ​Tuesday as investors assessed comments that indicated the Middle East conflict could escalate, ahead of President Donald Trump's deadline ‌for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fox to integrate Kalshi prediction market data across news platforms
item.description: Fox Corp said on Tuesday it will integrate Kalshi's ​prediction market data across its ‌news and streaming platforms, reflecting a broader trend among media organizations to ​use market-based probabilities to complement ​traditional reporting.

item.title: AI networking firm Aria Networks raises $125 million in funding
item.description: Aria Networks said on Tuesday it has raised $125 million in its first series funding round, as the startup ​seeks to develop its AI networking infrastructure to meet the ‌soaring demand for capacity amid the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence.

item.title: ASML shares fall on US Congress plan to further restrict China exports
item.description: Shares of computer chip equipment maker ASML dipped on Tuesday, the first trading day after U.S. lawmakers last week proposed a law that could further restrict the company's sales to ​China.

item.title: Morning Bid: Final countdown?
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Some firms in Saudi extend work from home ahead of Iran ultimatum
item.description: Some Western and Saudi firms in Riyadh have extended work-from-home advisories this week, five people familiar with the matter ​said, amid concern over a looming U.S.-imposed deadline for Iran ‌to open the Strait of Hormuz or face "hell".

item.title: UBS lowers 2026 S&P 500 target on Middle East conflict risks
item.description: UBS Global Wealth Management trimmed its S&P 500 index target for 2026, pointing to sustained higher oil ​prices amid the ongoing Middle East conflict that could pressure U.S. ‌economic growth and inflation.

item.title: Bourbon demand is down and tariffs aren't helping. But distillers keep building.
item.description: Like many whiskey distillers, Heaven Hill Brands is rolling back bourbon production this year, as demand lags. Yet, as the American spirit faced a tumultuous market in 2025, the Kentucky company built a new, $200 million distillery in the heart of bourbon country, adding 155,000 barrels of capacity.

item.title: Blockbuster SpaceX listing could suck the oxygen out of fragile IPO market
item.description: As Elon Musk's SpaceX closes in on a $75 billion IPO that could rewrite record books, concerns are mounting that others looking to list in 2026 may find it harder to get deals done under the shadow of the space venture's headline-grabbing debut.

item.title: Health insurers rise after US lifts 2027 Medicare Advantage payment rates
item.description: Shares of U.S. health insurers climbed in premarket trading on Tuesday after the government said it plans a ​larger-than-expected increase in 2027 payment rates for Medicare ‌Advantage plans.

item.title: Depressed tech valuations could offer entry point for investors, Goldman Sachs says
item.description: Technology stocks, including those from the United States, are looking cheap following a prolonged period of underperformance, creating a potential entry point for investors, Goldman ​Sachs said on Tuesday.

item.title: LG Energy Solution flags Q1 operating loss on weak EV demand
item.description: South Korean battery maker LG Energy Solution (LGES) said on Tuesday it expects to post a first-quarter operating loss of 208 billion ​won ($138.16 million), as weaker demand from electric vehicles (EVs) makers weighed ‌on earnings.

item.title: Ford recalls over 420,000 vehicles in US over windshield wiper failure
item.description: Ford Motor ​is recalling 422,613 vehicles ‌in the U.S. over a failure ​of the windshield ​wiper, the U.S. National ⁠Highway Traffic Safety ​Administration said on ​Tuesday.

item.title: Soaring costs, fuel shortage fears drive Pakistan to electric motorbikes
item.description: Days after Iran effectively blocked shipping through the Strait of Hormuz following the start of U.S. and Israeli attacks in late February, two Pakistani ​electric motorbike outlets 1,400 km (875 miles) away found themselves overwhelmed with enquiries.

item.title: Vietnam's MoMo weighs options including new investors at valuation above $2 billion, sources say
item.description: Vietnamese fintech firm MoMo is exploring strategic options, including ‌bringing in new investors, that could value the company at more than $2 billion, according to two sources with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Ackman's Pershing Square offers to buy Universal Music for nearly $65 billion
item.description: Bill Ackman's ​Pershing Square said ‌on Tuesday it proposed ​to buy ​Universal Music Group in ⁠a cash-and-stock ​deal worth about ​55.75 billion euros ($64.31 billion).

item.title: Morning Bid: A breakthrough deal or a crude awakening?
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee:

item.title: Chinese pigs fed new menu as Beijing weans farmers off US soy
item.description: At the edge of one of the many pig farms spread across the vast, unbroken floodplains of Taizhou, a two-hour drive northwest of ​Shanghai, a pair of square, four-metre pools of acrid-smelling ochre liquid hold the key to cutting costly soybean use in half.

item.title: China targets Taiwan's chip prowess to evade global 'containment', Taipei government says
item.description: China is targeting Taiwan to obtain its advanced chip manufacturing ​technology and talent as a way of breaking through international "containment" of the country, according ‌to a report from the island's top security agency.

item.title: Apple's foldable iPhone faces engineering snags, potential shipment delays, Nikkei Asia reports
item.description: Apple has been encountering setbacks ​in the engineering test phase of ‌its first foldable iPhone, which could lead to delays in its mass production and product shipment ​schedule, Nikkei Asia reported on Monday, ​citing sources.

item.title: Stocks struggle, oil jumps as Trump's Iran deadline looms
item.description: Global stocks wavered on Tuesday, while oil prices were perched near $110 per barrel as the prospect of ​escalation in the war in the Middle East and the looming deadline for a deal to be reached kept nervy investors ‌on the sidelines.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow
item.description: SpaceX outlined details of its highly anticipated IPO at a meeting with ‌its team of bankers Monday night, telling them it plans to earmark a large portion of shares for retail investors and will host 1,500 of them at an event in June following the IPO roadshow launch, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Deere settles US right-to-repair lawsuit with $99 million fund, repair commitments
item.description: U.S. agriculture equipment maker Deere on ​Monday agreed to pay $99 million into a settlement fund for ‌farms and farmers that are part of a class action over costs and access to repairs.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow, sources say
item.description: SpaceX outlined details of its highly anticipated IPO at a meeting with ​its team of bankers Monday night, telling ‌them it plans to earmark a large portion of shares for retail investors and will host 1,500 of them at ​an event in June following the IPO ​roadshow launch, according to two people familiar with ⁠the matter.

item.title: Dollar kept aloft as another Trump deadline looms
item.description: The dollar stood just shy of recent highs on Tuesday as traders counted down to a U.S.-imposed deadline for Iran to open the Persian Gulf to shipping or face attacks on its ​infrastructure.

item.title: US crude oil futures rise over $1 as Trump sharpens rhetoric on Iran
item.description: U.S. crude ​futures rose more ‌than $1 on Tuesday as ​U.S. President ​Donald Trump heightened ⁠his rhetoric ​against Iran, ​threatening stronger action if the country ​fails to ​reopen the critical ‌Strait ⁠of Hormuz.

item.title: Samsung flags eightfold jump in Q1 profit as AI chip demand drives up prices
item.description: Samsung Electronics on Tuesday projected a record-high first-quarter profit, up more than eightfold from a year earlier and well above expectations as booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure ​caused supply bottlenecks and drove chip prices higher.

item.title: Air NZ hikes fares, cuts flights as fuel costs more than double on Iran war
item.description: Air New Zealand said on Tuesday it would ​slash flights across May and June ‌as well as hike fares, as the Iran war disrupts global air travel and sends jet ​fuel costs higher.
